title: keyfile write not atomic — partial write leaves unrecoverable identity
description: cli/src/commands.rs:107-123: write_secret_file creates the file with create_new(true) then writes data. If the process is killed between create and write_all (or write_all fails partway), the file exists with partial/empty contents. The next run finds the file, tries to decrypt it, and fails with 'keyfile corrupt' — identity is permanently unrecoverable. Fix: write to a temp file (e.g. keyfile_path + '.tmp') with the same 0600 mode, fsync, then rename atomically. POSIX rename is atomic; this reduces the corruption window to near zero.
